channel.title: Computational Material Science
channel.description: Molecules to crystal - Design and predict

item.title: Getting started
item.description:
first steps in modeling

Python (Best for scripting, data analysis, and visualization)

Python and its data analysis and visualization libraries (scipy, numpy, matplotlib, pandas, scikit-learn, etc.) is the best choice for post-processing and representation of your research. Python is free, has a big community and you can find a large number of projects related to your work there.

Basics of Density functional theory

As the main workhorse for computational material science, DFT yields a relatively exact description from different properties of the system at quantum-level.

Solid state/Quantum chemistry packages

A comprehensive list of quantum chemistry and solid-state physics software is provided below:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_quantum_chemistry_and_solid-state_physics_software

From we will focus mainly on some of mostly used packages suck as G09, NWchem, SIESTA, gpaw, and Quantum-ESPRESSO.

Why ASE?

“The Atomic Simulation Environment (ASE) is a set of tools and Python modules for setting up, manipulating, running, visualizing and analyzing atomistic simulations.”

item.title: Welcome to CMS
item.description:
This page is created by Yavar T Azar, to collect some computational techniques in quantum chemistry and solid-state.

I am working in computational material science since 2012, and this page is a brief collection from my technical experiences in researching material science field using numerical methods.
